question: Is the final answer presented as a sequence of valid directional instructions (e.g., 'down, down, right, down, left') using only allowed moves (up, down, left, right)?
Did the model's final path successfully connect the Start point (S) to the End point (E) without any breaks or discontinuities?
Does the final path sequence exactly match the optimal reference solution: 'down, down, right, down, left'?
Is the final path length exactly 5 moves, matching the shortest path length for this maze?
When the text sequence is simulated step-by-step on the maze grid, does it avoid crossing or passing through any black wall segments?
Did the model generate at least one image visually depicting the path or reasoning process?
In the generated images, is the drawn path clearly distinguishable from the maze walls and background (e.g., using a contrasting color or line thickness)?
In the generated images, is the drawn path strictly contained within the open corridors, avoiding any overlap with or crossing of black wall lines?
Does the drawn path in the image correctly originate at the Green 'S' zone and terminate at the Red 'E' zone?
If multiple intermediate images are generated, does the underlying maze structure (walls, S position, E position) remain unchanged across all images?
If multiple intermediate images are generated, does the path build progressively from the previous step without teleporting or skipping cells?
Does the model demonstrate understanding of the maze's grid-based movement constraints (one cell at a time, four cardinal directions only, no diagonals)?
Does the model correctly identify that the first two moves must be 'down' to navigate the vertical corridor starting from S?
Does the model correctly identify that a 'right' turn is required after the initial descent to avoid the horizontal wall blocking the path south?
Does the model correctly identify that after moving right, the next required move is 'down' to approach the target row?
Does the model correctly conclude the path with a 'left' turn to enter the 'E' cell?
Do the textual direction instructions perfectly match the sequence of segments drawn in the image (e.g., text says 'right', image shows a line to the right)?
Does the number of directional moves stated in the text match the number of cell transitions visible in the path image (5 moves)?
Are there any text-image contradictions, such as the text claiming to avoid a wall while the image shows the path crossing it?
